title: t-strings: templateiter_next advances a shared TemplateIter's from_strings flag + sub-iterators non-atomically (Objects/templateobject.c) -- value-benign
description: The PEP 750 t-string template iterator (templateiterobject in Objects/templateobject.c, from iter(t'...')) alternates between the template's strings and interpolations, tracking which via a plain self->from_strings int and advancing self->stringsiter / self->interpolationsiter. templateiter_next writes self->from_strings (:25/:31/:36) and PyIter_Next's the shared sub-iterators with no synchronization. A shared TemplateIter driven by multiple threads races the from_strings flag and the sub-iterator cursors -> value-benign data race (wrong item order / duplicate / skip / StopIteration).
race_kind: shared-iterator state: non-atomic read/write of a template iterator's from_strings flag and its sub-iterator cursors -> value-benign data race. Same class as the value-benign builtin iterators (gh-120496 / gh-124397).
shared_state: templateiterobject.from_strings (int flag) + the shared stringsiter/interpolationsiter cursors on a single shared TemplateIter
severity: low (value-benign): a mis-ordered / duplicated / skipped item under concurrency; no memory unsafety (the Py_SETREF is on a local `item`, not shared state). Per gh-124397 (iterator FT strategy) such concurrent-iterator value races are acceptable.
repro_reliability: Magalu fleet surfaced the templateiter_next self-race (1 veh). repro.py (8 threads sharing one iter(t'...')) did NOT trip TSan in a quick isolated run -- a short template exhausts before the workers contend; a longer template / more interpolations is needed to widen the window. Not independently reproduced this session.
suggested_fix: Per gh-124397 this value-benign class is acceptable (a shared iterator should be locked by the user). If ever hardened: atomic from_strings + per-iterator critical section over the sub-iterator advance.
prior_art: gh api search found no template-iterator FT issue -> appears unfiled, but it is the value-benign shared-iterator class (gh-120496 closed as acceptable per gh-124397). New iterator TYPE (PEP 750 t-strings, 3.14+), same defect shape.
related: Value-benign shared-iterator class, like seq/deque (TSAN-0044), set (TSAN-0040). The t-string template iterator is a new member. Surfaced in a downloaded remote fleet.
notes: NEW iterator type but VALUE-BENIGN (gh-120496/gh-124397 class) -> not fileable. Cataloged for dedup. Crash-checked negative (the Py_SETREF is on a local, not shared state, so no double-free like ga_iternext/TSAN-0045).
